name: Scythian Gold Deer

visual_attributes.morphology: crouching deer with legs tucked beneath its body, head upright
distinctive_features: muscles tight to give the impression of speed
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: varies from small plaques to large shield ornaments
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: central ornament for shields or smaller plaques for clothing

domain_specific_functional.cultural_significance: possibly a clan totem for steppes peoples
domain_specific_functional.use_in_art: central ornament for shields, clothing decoration

relationships.description: The Scythian Gold Deer is a product of Scythian art and culture.
Some of the most notable examples are housed in the Hermitage Museum.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Scythian territories, including Kuban, Hungary, Crimea
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Highly valued by museums, possibly a clan totem

contrastive_constraints: NOT modern or contemporary art style
